channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: XPENG GX Introduces In-Car Fridge and Central Air System
item.description: XPENG has opened pre-orders for its GX flagship SUV, featuring a $56,000 starting price and a first-of-its-kind sterile in-car refrigerator co-developed with Midea, alongside a high-efficiency central air system.

item.title: AGILINK Launches OmniHand 3 Series, Ships Over 8,000 Dexterous Hands
item.description: AGILINK, a spin-off from AGIBOT, has unveiled its OmniHand 3 series of dexterous robotic hands, including a sub-$150 lightweight model and a 5kg-payload flagship, with over 8,000 units already deployed in industrial settings.

item.title: Dreame Maintains 100 Percent Annual Growth Over Eight Years
item.description: Dreame Technology has achieved a remarkable 100% compound annual revenue growth rate for eight straight years, securing the top market share position for robot vacuums in over 30 countries globally.

item.title: WeiMai Files Updated Prospectus for Hong Kong IPO, Targets AI Healthcare Leadership
item.description: WeiMai Inc., an AI-driven full-cycle disease management platform, has filed an updated Hong Kong IPO prospectus, reporting $121 million in 2025 revenue and a near-breakeven adjusted net loss as its hospital partnerships expand.

item.title: Motorevo Raises USD Tens of Millions in A++ Round, Sixth Funding in One Year
item.description: Motorevo, a Chinese integrated robotic joint module supplier, has raised tens of millions of USD in an A++ round—its sixth financing in under a year—to scale production capacity toward 500,000 units by 2026.

item.title: CISA Adds 8 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets April-May 2026 Federal Deadlines
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added eight new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, including three flaws impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, citing evidence of active exploitation. The list of vulnerabilities is as...

item.title: Degraded network performance for some users around Dallas, Texas
item.description: Apr 21, 05:27 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 21, 05:11 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 21, 04:58 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 21, 04:58 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is...

item.title: KWBench: Measuring Unprompted Problem Recognition in Knowledge Work
item.description: arXiv:2604.15760v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce the first version of KWBench (Knowledge Work Bench), a benchmark for unprompted problem recognition in large language models: can an LLM identify a professional scenario before attempting to solve it. Existing frontier benchmarks have...

item.title: Stein Variational Black-Box Combinatorial Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2604.15837v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Combinatorial black-box optimization in high-dimensional settings demands a careful trade-off between exploiting promising regions of the search space and preserving sufficient exploration to identify multiple optima. Although...

item.title: Discover and Prove: An Open-source Agentic Framework for Hard Mode Automated Theorem Proving in Lean 4
item.description: arXiv:2604.15839v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Most ATP benchmarks embed the final answer within the formal statement -- a convention we call "Easy Mode" -- a design that simplifies the task relative to what human competitors face and may lead to optimistic estimates of model capability. We call...

item.title: Experience Compression Spectrum: Unifying Memory, Skills, and Rules in LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.15877v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As LLM agents scale to long-horizon, multi-session deployments, efficiently managing accumulated experience becomes a critical bottleneck. Agent memory systems and agent skill discovery both address this challenge -- extracting reusable knowledge from...

item.title: Towards Rigorous Explainability by Feature Attribution
item.description: arXiv:2604.15898v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: For around a decade, non-symbolic methods have been the option of choice when explaining complex machine learning (ML) models. Unfortunately, such methods lack rigor and can mislead human decision-makers. In high-stakes uses of ML, the lack of rigor...

item.title: BASIS: Balanced Activation Sketching with Invariant Scalars for "Ghost Backpropagation"
item.description: arXiv:2604.16324v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The activation memory required for exact backpropagation scales linearly with network depth, context length, and feature dimensionality, forming an O(L * BN ) spatial bottleneck (where B is the sequence-batch cardinality and N is the feature...

item.title: UniMamba: A Unified Spatial-Temporal Modeling Framework with State-Space and Attention Integration
item.description: arXiv:2604.16325v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multivariate time series forecasting is fundamental to numerous domains such as energy, finance, and environmental monitoring, where complex temporal dependencies and cross-variable interactions pose enduring challenges. Existing Transformer-based...

item.title: Annotation Entropy Predicts Per-Example Learning Dynamics in LoRA Fine-Tuning
item.description: arXiv:2604.16332v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We find that LoRA fine-tuning exhibits un-learning on contested examples: items with high annotator disagreement show increasing loss during training, a qualitatively distinct pattern largely absent under full fine-tuning and consistent across all six...

item.title: A Discordance-Aware Multimodal Framework with Multi-Agent Clinical Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.16333v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Knee osteoarthritis frequently exhibits discordance between structural damage observed in imaging and patient-reported symptoms such as pain. This mismatch complicates clinical interpretation and patient stratification and remains insufficiently...

item.title: Preventing overfitting in deep learning using differential privacy
item.description: arXiv:2604.16334v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The use of Deep Neural Network based systems in the real world is growing. They have achieved state-of-the-art performance on many image, speech and text datasets. They have been shown to be powerful systems that are capable of learning detailed...

item.title: Multimodal Claim Extraction for Fact-Checking
item.description: arXiv:2604.16311v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automated Fact-Checking (AFC) relies on claim extraction as a first step, yet existing methods largely overlook the multimodal nature of today's misinformation. Social media posts often combine short, informal text with images such as memes,...

item.title: Cross-Family Speculative Decoding for Polish Language Models on Apple~Silicon: An Empirical Evaluation of Bielik~11B with UAG-Extended MLX-LM
item.description: arXiv:2604.16368v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates LLM inference by using a small draft model to propose k candidate tokens for a target model to verify. While effective for same-tokenizer pairs on high-bandwidth GPUs, its applicability to cross-family pairs with...

item.title: Brain-CLIPLM: Decoding Compressed Semantic Representations in EEG for Language Reconstruction
item.description: arXiv:2604.16370v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decoding natural language from non-invasive electroencephalography (EEG) remains fundamentally limited by low signal-to-noise ratio and restricted information bandwidth. This raises a fundamental question regarding whether sentence-level linguistic...

item.title: CFMS: Towards Explainable and Fine-Grained Chinese Multimodal Sarcasm Detection Benchmark
item.description: arXiv:2604.16372v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal sarcasm detection has recently garnered significant attention. However, existing benchmarks suffer from coarse-grained annotations and limited cultural coverage, which hinder research into fine-grained semantic understanding. To address...

item.title: Foundational Study on Authorship Attribution of Japanese Web Reviews for Actor Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.16376v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study investigates the applicability of authorship attribution based on stylistic features to support actor analysis in threat intelligence. As a foundational step toward future application to dark web forums, we conducted experiments using...

item.title: Increased HTTP 5xx Errors in Sydney
item.description: Apr 21, 03:25 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of HTTP 5xx errors in Sydney, NSW, Australia - (SYD).We are working to analyse and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Some users may encounter issues with GPT-5.4-C model in Codex
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Codex API (Degraded performance) Codex Web (Degraded performance) CLI (Degraded performance) App (Degraded performance) VS Code extension (Degraded performance)

item.title: How to Ground a Korean AI Agent in Real Demographics with Synthetic Personas
[AINews] Moonshot Kimi K2.6: the world's leading Open Model refreshes to catch up to Opus 4.6 (ahead of DeepSeek v4?)
item.description: Yay Kimi!!!

item.title: Some users will see higher error rates on Codex
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.

item.title: Elevated errors for ChatGPT conversations in Europe
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Reading today's open-closed performance gap
item.description: The complex factors that determine the single evaluation number so many focus on. Plus, how this changes in the future.

item.title: A Note on TurboQuant and the Earlier DRIVE/EDEN Line of Work
item.description: This note clarifies the relationship between the recent TurboQuant work and the earlier DRIVE (NeurIPS 2021) and EDEN (ICML 2022) schemes. DRIVE is a 1-bit quantizer that EDEN extended to any $b>0$ bits per coordinate; we refer to them collectively as EDEN. First, TurboQuant$_{\text{mse}}$ is a...

item.title: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) Enables RCE via Malicious GGUF Model Files
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in SGLang that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution on susceptible systems. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5760, carries a CVSS score of 9.8 out of 10.0. It has been described as a case of command injection...

item.title: Users unable to load ChatGPT, Codex and API Platform
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Agent (Operational) Connectors/Apps (Operational) Voice mode (Operational) Search (Operational) Compliance API (Operational) Image Generation (Operational) Deep Research (Operational) ChatGPT Atlas...

item.title: 🔬 Training Transformers to solve 95% failure rate of Cancer Trials — Ron Alfa & Daniel Bear, Noetik
item.description: 95% of cancer treatments fail to pass clinical trials, but it may be a matching problem — that Noetik is solving with autoregressive transformers like TARIO-2!

item.title: Serial-to-IP Converter Flaws Expose OT and Healthcare Systems to Hacking
item.description: Forescout researchers discovered 20 new vulnerabilities in Lantronix and Silex products and described theoretical attack scenarios. The post Serial-to-IP Converter Flaws Expose OT and Healthcare Systems to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Vercel Hack, Push Fraud, QEMU Abused, New Android RATs Emerge & More
item.description: Monday’s recap shows the same pattern in different places. A third-party tool becomes a way in, then leads to internal access. A trusted download path is briefly swapped to deliver malware. Browser extensions act normally while pulling data and running code. Even update channels are used to push...

item.title: Elevated errors for uploading files
item.description: Apr 20, 13:31 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating an issue related to uploading files on Claude.ai and Claude API. Some users will see their uploads failing and they are advised to retry.

item.title: DocQAC: Adaptive Trie-Guided Decoding for Effective In-Document Query Auto-Completion
item.description: Query auto-completion (QAC) has been widely studied in the context of web search, yet remains underexplored for in-document search, which we term DocQAC. DocQAC aims to enhance search productivity within long documents by helping users craft faster, more precise queries, even for complex or...

item.title: Partial degradation for code scanning default setup and for code quality
item.description: Apr 20, 13:28 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of impacted performance for some GitHub services.

item.title: Horospherical Depth and Busemann Median on Hadamard Manifolds
item.description: \We introduce the horospherical depth, an intrinsic notion of statistical depth on Hadamard manifolds, and define the Busemann median as the set of its maximizers. The construction exploits the fact that the linear functionals appearing in Tukey's half-space depth are themselves limits of...

item.title: DiffuSAM: Diffusion Guided Zero-Shot Object Grounding for Remote Sensing Imagery
item.description: Diffusion models have emerged as powerful tools for a wide range of vision tasks, including text-guided image generation and editing. In this work, we explore their potential for object grounding in remote sensing imagery. We propose a hybrid pipeline that integrates diffusion-based localization...

item.title: British Scattered Spider Hacker Pleads Guilty in the US
item.description: Tyler Buchanan admitted in court to hacking into various companies, defrauding them, and stealing cryptocurrency from multiple individuals. The post British Scattered Spider Hacker Pleads Guilty in the US appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: murderous ‘mirror’ bacteria, and Chinese workers fighting AI doubles
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. No one’s sure if synthetic mirror life will kill us all In February 2019, a group of scientists proposed a high-risk, cutting-edge, irresistibly...

item.title: Preparing for severe cyber threat: why leaders must act now
item.description: A call to action to collectively build UK resilience.

item.title: Hackers Abuse QEMU for Defense Evasion
item.description: The machine emulator has been abused in at least two different campaigns distributing ransomware and remote access tools. The post Hackers Abuse QEMU for Defense Evasion appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Why Most AI Deployments Stall After the Demo
item.description: The fastest way to fall in love with an AI tool is to watch the demo. Everything moves quickly. Prompts land cleanly. The system produces impressive outputs in seconds. It feels like the beginning of a new era for your team. But most AI initiatives don't fail because of bad technology. They stall...

item.title: Rulesets Rate Limiting accuracy
item.description: Apr 20, 11:08 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 20, 10:13 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare is aware of, and investigating an issue where some requests processed by workers may not be correctly enforcing rulesets rate limiting counts.

item.title: Bluesky Disrupted by Sophisticated DDoS Attack
item.description: A pro-Iran hacker group has taken credit for the attack on Bluesky, which appears to have lasted 24 hours. The post Bluesky Disrupted by Sophisticated DDoS Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Anthropic MCP Design Vulnerability Enables RCE, Threatening AI Supply Chain
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a critical "by design" weakness in the Model Context Protocol's (MCP) architecture that could pave the way for remote code execution and have a cascading effect on the artificial intelligence (AI) supply chain. "This flaw enables Arbitrary Command Execution...

item.title: Senate Extends Surveillance Powers Until April 30 After Chaotic Votes in House
item.description: The Senate approved a short-term renewal until April 30 of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies. The post Senate Extends Surveillance Powers Until April 30 After Chaotic Votes in House appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Colossal Biosciences said it cloned red wolves. Is it for real?
item.description: If you want to capture something wolflike, it’s best to embark before dawn. So on a morning this January, with the eastern horizon still pink-hued, I drove with two young scientists into a blanket of fog. Forty miles to the west, the industrial sprawl of Houston spawned a golden glow. Tanner...
